sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.10 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: A group$G$is called locally indicable\/ if every non-trivial finitely generated subgroup of$G$has an infinite cyclic factor group. Is every torsion-free one-relator group locally indicable? { G.\,Baumslag} Yes, it is (S.\,D.\,Brodski , Dep. no.\,2214-80, VINITI, Moscow, 1980 (Russian)).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
